mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during tightening phases compared to neutral phases (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant positive skew relative to the baseline, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls increase by more than 150,000 in a single month, it confirms robust economic momentum that sustains equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where commercial and industrial loan growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals excessive credit expansion that eventually triggers a liquidity crunch, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to show statistical significance in a panel regression, or forward returns do not decline when RSAFS rises and FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production peaks and unemployment remains below 5.0%, supply constraints amplify inflationary pressures, forcing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO peaks while UNRATE stays below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 3-year rolling window.
